title: cypholophus trapula
intro: Cypholophus trapula is a type of plant native to the Pacific Islands, specifically belonging to the family Lamiaceae and known for its medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory congestion, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. In traditional medicine, Cypholophus trapula is prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the leaves are steeped in hot water to create a herbal remedy for its medicinal uses.
